# Course Overview {.unnumbered}

Welcome to the advanced clinical research analytics course for PhD-level
learners. This course is designed to move beyond introductory R and
focus on research-ready data science practices, reproducible workflows,
and tidyverse-based statistical analysis.

Built for researchers handling real-world clinical data, this course
focuses on modern data management, rigorous analysis, and reproducible
reporting. The material is arranged into modules that reflect the
research lifecycle:

- Fundamentals of reproducible research and project management
- Tidy data wrangling with dplyr and tidyr
- Advanced visualization and scientific communication
- Clinical research modeling and survival analysis
- Reproducible pipelines and scalable research workflows

## What You Will Learn {.unnumbered}

- Modern tidyverse workflows for data cleaning and analysis.
- Reproducible project structure with Quarto and `renv`.
- Advanced clinical research methods, including survival analysis.
- High-quality scientific visualization with ggplot2.
- How to embed research logic into reproducible reports.
- Practical case studies drawing from real medical and clinical data.

## Why This Course {.unnumbered}

This course is not a beginner tutorial; it is designed for PhD students
and researchers who need to apply R to real research problems. You will
learn how to manage data, perform robust analyses, and document findings
in a reproducible way.

R is the leading language for statistical science and academic research.
With the tidyverse ecosystem, you can perform complex data manipulation
and visualization in a way that is both expressive and reproducible.

# Aims {#Aims}

1. Introduce modern reproducible research workflows in R.
2. Teach advanced tidyverse-based data manipulation.
3. Develop publication-quality visualization and reporting skills.
4. Present practical clinical research analytics techniques.
5. Build an integrated, reproducible research project pipeline.

# Learning Objectives {#Learning-Objectives}

Upon completing this course, you will be able to:

1. Structure and manage research projects reproducibly in R.
2. Use dplyr, tidyr, and purrr to clean and shape complex datasets.
3. Create high-quality scientific graphics with ggplot2.
4. Apply R Markdown / Quarto for reproducible reports and documents.
5. Work with clinical and survival analysis data using appropriate
   methods.
6. Automate analysis workflows and maintain reproducible code.

# Prerequisites {#Prerequisites}

- Comfort with basic statistics and research study concepts.
- Familiarity with the RStudio interface is helpful but not required.
- A computer with internet access and the ability to install R packages.

Please follow the installation and environment setup instructions in the
course materials before beginning the exercises.
